item.title: Daybreak Star Community 5K Run and Walk
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

Join the community for a series of monthly 5K runs and walks held at the historic Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center. This free event welcomes participants of all levels\, from competitive runners to those who enjoy a leisurely walk through beautiful scenery. Event Schedule Events take place on the 3rd Sunday of each month. Please note the following upcoming dates: February 15th March 15th April 19th May 17th June 21st July 19th (37th Annual Indian Days Powwow Weekend\, starting at 9:00 AM) Timing and Location Details Start Time: 10:00 AM Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way Seattle\, WA 98199) Meeting Point: Meet at the Powwow Grounds This event is FREE for all! Lace up your shoes and enjoy the scenic trails while connecting with the community! For more information about the venue and organizing group\, please email communications@unitedindians.org

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Paving the Way: Bicycle Infrastructure Tour
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: Offsite

Join Merlin Rainwater\, creator of Seattle’s Infamous Redline Guide\, and Tom Fucoloro\, Founder and Editor of the Seattle Bike Blog and author of Biking Uphill in the Rain: The Story of Seattle from behind the Handlebars\, for a bicycle tour exploring the history of Seattle bicycle infrastructure. \nThis tour requires riding a bicycle between multiple sites in Seattle\, totaling close to 10 miles and starting in the Central District. Please bring your own bike. Helmets and lights will be required. For any accessibility support\, or general inquiries\, email programs@mohai.org.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Veteran Benefits 101 Workshop Series
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Labateyah Youth Home\, 9010 13th Ave NW\, Seattle\, WA\, 98117\, United States

Our Native Veterans & Elders Programs are teaming up with the WA’s VA to bring you a Veteran Benefits 101 Workshop series! Whether you are looking to learn about your eligibility or ready to start an application onsite with an accredited Veteran Service Officer\, we are here to support you! Share this workshop series with the veterans in your community! Event Details When: The last Friday of each month Time: 11 am to 1 pm Where: Labateyah Youth Home\, 9010 13th Ave NW\, Seattle\, WA 98117 Upcoming Workshop Topics March 27: Veterans Benefits Administration 101 (Disability Compensation\, Education\, Home Loans\, Burials\, & more) April 24: Veterans Health Administration 101 (VA Hospital & Medical Care) May 29: Veteran Service Officer Networking & Additional Benefits 101 What to Expect Lunch provided for attendees Gift cards available for veterans\, veteran spouses\, or caretakers of veterans (Limit 1 per household) Transportation is available\, if needed Registration is encouraged to help us prepare: https://forms.office.com/r/KrRTQ86ZPr   Questions? Please email us at veterans@unitedindians.org Note: Workshop topics and schedules are subject to change. #SeattleVeterans #SeattleEvents #WashingtonVA #SeattleSeniors #VeteranBenefits #VABenefits #ServiceOfficers #VeteranSupport #MilitaryFamily #Veterans101 #VeteranResources

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Kickstands Up! All Motorcycle Club Day
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: MOHAI\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Inviting all motorcycle clubs to roll on down to MOHAI! Indicate your Motorcycle Cub membership and receive $5 off admission all day\, and explore Kickstands Up: 125 Years of Motorcycling in the Pacific Northwest with gallery talks by exhibit co-curators from the Pacific Northwest Museum of Motorcycling from 2-4pm. \nSpots are limited and are assigned on a first-come\, first-served basis. \nFor accessibility support\, please contact programs@mohai.org two weeks before the event.

item.title: Forest Garden Land & Community Tending Days
item.description:
Thursday, April 30, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

Help us grow this March during Forest Garden Land & Community Tending Days at the Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center! 🌱✨ \nCome support Indigenous food sovereignty by tending to traditional plants like camas and huckleberry. The plants grown here nourishes our elders\, youth\, families\, and ceremonies. Help us build a future rooted in culture\, care\, and connection! 🍓 \nQuestions? Email Shameka.Gagnier@gmail.com or call/text (720) 212 7113 📞

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, April 30, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Interbay Summer Camps- The Little Gym
item.description:
Friday, May 1, 2026 at 12 AM

Location: The Little Gym- Interbay\, 2213 15th Ave W\, Seattle\, WA\, 98119\, United States

Interbay summer camps are 3 hours of Serious Fun for kiddos 3-10 years of age! Camp consists of 2 hours of gym time\, and 1 hour of snacks and craft time. Every week brings a new and exciting theme for your camper and we always have blocks\, books and coloring pages on standby for those campers that need a break. Camps are held Monday-Wednesday from 1-4pm. We offer special discounted pricing for members and camp pack purchases. Non member pricing is $75 for a single camp pass. More information on pack pricing can be found via our scannable QR code. Priority registration opens February 1st.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: Powwow Dance Classes
item.description:
Thursday, May 7, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Daybreak Star Indian Cultural Center\, 5011 Bernie Whitebear Way\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

We are proud to announce that Powwow Dance Classes have returned! This 19 week program is open to all Indigenous families and individuals. Participants will dive deep into the protocols: history: and cultural significance of the Powwow. This series offers a unique opportunity for dancers to be honored during a special (optional) Coming Out Ceremony at the 37th Annual Indian Days Powwow. Workshop Details 📅 Dates: Thursdays from February 19th through July 2nd (UPDATE: There will be class on March 12\, 2026) ⏰ Time: 6:30 PM to 8:30 PM 📍 Location: Daybreak Star Cultural Center (5011 Bernie Whitebear Way: Seattle: WA) 🍎 Refreshments provided! Dance Styles Taught Mens’ Styles: Grass: Fancy: and Traditional Womens’ Styles: Jingle: Fancy: and Traditional Meet the Instructors Lauren Nabahe (Paiute Shoshone: Navajo: Mono) Jobey T. Williams (Tulalip and Yakama) Contact and More Information For questions or further details: please reach out via email at: Events@unitedindians.org View the event flyer here: https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1KSaA6TiNb/

From United Indians / Daybreak Star

item.title: Sensory Friendly Morning
item.description:
Saturday, May 9, 2026 at 9 AM

Location: MOHAI\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

This event welcomes adults and families who may benefit from a low-sensory environment to explore the museum. During this event\, the museum will be closed to the general public and the lights and sounds in the gallery will be lowered. A sensory map and information about what to expect during your visit to MOHAI will be provided via email following registration. Spots are limited so register early! \nHoras más tranquilas\nFecha/hora: sábado 9 de mayo\, de 9:00 a. m. a 11:00 a. m.\nCosto: gratuito con inscripción previa \nEste evento da la bienvenida a adultos y familias que puedan beneficiarse de un entorno con bajo nivel sensorial para explorar el museo. Durante el evento\, el museo permanecerá cerrado al público en general\, y se bajarán las luces y los sonidos de la galería. Tras la inscripción\, se enviará por correo electrónico un mapa sensorial e información sobre lo que puede esperar durante su visita al Museum of History & Industry (MOHAI\, Museo de Historia e Industria). Los cupos son limitados\, así que inscríbase pronto.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

item.title: Meet Up Magnolia- virtual networking
item.description:
Thursday, May 14, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/85388228818?pwd=amdmUEMvcVo2YjhETkxkbGJCa1Q4Zz09

Our member businesses know the value of networking. Join us for our virtual networking event\, Meet Up Magnolia\, on the  2nd Thursday of every month. \nHow does this virtual networking work? You simply log on to the Zoom link and will be paired into one-on-one chats for 8 to 10 minutes each. We will repeat the process for approximately 3 or 4 rounds. You even have the option to follow up after the meeting and send a message to one or more of the people you meet to keep the dialogue going. \nSo many people to meet\, so many professional relationships to build. Join us HERE.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: Behind the Seams: Roaring '20s
item.description:
Saturday, May 16, 2026 at 11 AM

Location: Norcliffe Conference Room\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Behind the Seams is back\, with a closer look at the artifacts than ever before! One hundred years later\, women’s fashions of the 1920s continue to shock and to dazzle. Hemlines rose up to the knee and eveningwear were built to move\, shake\, and sparkle. Join Curator of Collections\, Clara Berg for a historical overview and then an intimate viewing of the most stunning 1920s dresses from the museum collection. \nA member pre-sale for this event will begin on March 11 and run until March 23. Secure your spot early by becoming a MOHAI member today! \nFor accessibility support\, contact programs@mohai.org two weeks before the program.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

Event details

item.title: History Café: Field Notes Podcast
item.description:
Wednesday, May 20, 2026 at 6:30 PM

Location: Faye G. Allen Grand Atrium\, MOHAI 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Join HistoryLink’s Tony Williams for a live recording of the Field Notes podcast featuring UW Architecture Professor Vikram Prakash. We’re digging into the intersection of deep time and urban design to discover how Seattle’s geological past continues to shape its future. \nHistory Café is produced as a partnership between HistoryLink\, The Pacific Northwest Historians Guild\, and MOHAI. \nASL Interpretation and CART captioning are available during the program. In addition\, a limited number of Assistive Listening Devices are available upon request. For more accessibility support\, email programs@mohai.org before the program. \nGrab some food for thought–complimentary snacks and beverages will be provided. \nIf you can’t make it to the museum\, tune in live on the MOHAI YouTube channel to virtually attend this event.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

item.title: Drawing From History: Ripples of Time
item.description:
Saturday, May 30, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: Microsoft Lakefront Pavilion\, MOHAI - 860 Terry Ave N\, Seattle\, WA\, 98109\, United States

Lake Union is haunted by ghosts of the past\, echoing through time. In this class\, participants will explore past\, present\, and future of the Lake Union Watershed by creating quick layered gesture drawings in charcoal\, based off photos and ephemera from MOHAI’s collections. The class will culminate at the water’s edge\, creating a drawing representing visions for the future. \nDrawing From History is offered in partnership with Gage Academy of Art. \nAll art making materials will be provided. Tickets include access to MOHAI’s exhibitions. For accessibility support and general program inquiries\, email programs@mohai.org.

From MOHAI - Museum of History & Industry

item.title: Business & Breakfast
item.description:
Tuesday, June 9, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Aegis Living at Rodgers Park\, 2900 3rd Ave W\, Seattle\, WA\, 98119\, United States

Our Business & Breakfast series brings local professionals together for meaningful networking\, practical business education\, and great conversation. Each session features a guest speaker or facilitator offering tools you can use right away—whether you’re growing your business\, strengthening your team\, or expanding your local connections. Join us\, enjoy a chef-prepared breakfast\, and start your morning with inspiration and community.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: Meet Up Magnolia- virtual networking
item.description:
Thursday, June 11, 2026 at 9:30 AM

Location: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/85388228818?pwd=amdmUEMvcVo2YjhETkxkbGJCa1Q4Zz09

Our member businesses know the value of networking. Join us for our virtual networking event\, Meet Up Magnolia\, on the  2nd Thursday of every month. \nHow does this virtual networking work? You simply log on to the Zoom link and will be paired into one-on-one chats for 8 to 10 minutes each. We will repeat the process for approximately 3 or 4 rounds. You even have the option to follow up after the meeting and send a message to one or more of the people you meet to keep the dialogue going. \nSo many people to meet\, so many professional relationships to build. Join us HERE.

From Discover Magnolia

item.title: Magnolia Flea Market
item.description:
Saturday, June 13, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Magnolia Flea Market\, 4257 24th Ave W\, Seattle\, WA\, 98199\, United States

Discover a curated mix of vintage finds\, local makers\, handmade goods\, sweet treats\, coffee\, and food—plus a vintage warehouse full of gems to explore. \nWhether you’re hunting for one-of-a-kind pieces or just enjoying the Magnolia neighborhood\, this is your weekend spot. Bring a friend\, bring your pup\, and come hang out.
